Biography

Alin Gherman

Born in 1981 in Transylvania, Romania, Alin Gherman moved to Belgium at age 11 and began to play music and compose as an autodidact.

He followed composition lessons at the Conservatoire Royal de Bruxelles with Daniel Capelletti and at the Akademia Muzyczna w Krakowie (Poland) with Zbigniew Bargielski, Marek Choloniewski (electronic studio SME) and Rafal Delekta (conducting). He received a scholarship from the Swiss Confederation to study instrumental theatre with Georges Aperghis, Xavier Dayer, Daniel Ott, Eric Gaudibert and Pierre Sublet at the Hochschule der Künste Bern. He also completed the Master in Composition with a minor in Film Music at the Zürcher Hochschule der Künste with Domenico Ferrari.

He participated in masterclasses in Weimar, Český Krumlov, Breaza, as well as Acanthes (Metz), Tactus (Brussels–Mons), SYPEMeda International Stage for Young Performers and Media Artists (Minsk) and multimedia experimental workshops "x-med-k" of Nadine/FoAM (Brussels).

Commissions included works for the Tonhalle Zürich, Concours de Genève, Pro Helvetia, European Contemporary Orchestra and Ensemble Musiques Nouvelles. He composed the soundtrack of the silent film "Ein Werktag", performed live at the Winterthur Short Film Festival 2009.

Winner of the YCP Competition at the Beijing Modern Music Festival 2011 and Icon Arts Romania 2005. Honourable acknowledgement of the Universität Oldenburg. Published by Editions Papillon, Geneva.

Crafting metal spoons chimes at H.K.B. Workshop, Bern, Switzerland

Instrument Availability

Select instruments are available for professional use through the Centre International de Percussion, Geneva, Switzerland. All other instruments remain the property of the composer.

Click to see more details

Wood Chimes

Custom wooden percussion creating warm, organic tones

Stone Chimes

Natural stone with unique resonant qualities

Metal Spoons Chimes

Handcrafted metallic percussion instruments

Metal Cans Chimes

Recycled metal creating distinctive timbres

Metal Tubes Chimes

Precisely tuned tubes for ethereal sounds

Glass Chimes

Delicate glass producing crystalline tones
